document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical Evaluation Plan A-TCF-5-063 Rev.2 for the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector under CE/MDD clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to plan clinical evaluation, demonstrate equivalence, assess safety and performance, and support CE marking conformity activities for ADD.
keyPoints: ADD is identified as a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, model name ADD, classified Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film based radiographic diagnostic systems, and it is not to be used for mammography.
The clinical evaluation is described as part of the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2012 and in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4.
The plan relies on literature review, substantially equivalent predicate device data, manufacturer-held clinical data, PMS, PMCF, and safety and performance test information.
Sales approval status for South Korea, EU, U.S.A., and China is listed as TBD.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation lifecycle
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is regarded as an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2012.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: Equivalence demonstration will be asserted in terms of three characteristics (clinical, technical, and biological).

concepts.name: Essential Requirements
concepts.evidence: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed

concepts.name: Post Market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: The PMS report includes: Customer Complaints Analysis (Latest 5 years) - Adverse Events - Experience with Similar Devices Made by the Same or Different Manufacturer - Literature Reviews - PMCF Study

concepts.name: Post Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: Through continuous PMS activity... PMCF studies will be conducted in accordance with the PMCF Plan if necessary.

concepts.name: Digital radiography
concepts.evidence: Digital radiography offers a number of new capabilities such as post-processing, electronic archival, concurrent access to the images, and improved image distribution.

evidence.claim: The document is Clinical Evaluation Plan A-TCF-5-063 Rev.2 dated 2021.04.02.
evidence.evidenceText: Doc. No. A-TCF-5-063 Rev. No. 2 Date 2021.04.02

evidence.claim: The device under evaluation is ADD, a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name ADD
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd. Address 1F, 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: ADD is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital flat panel x-ray detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: ADD is contraindicated for mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target user group includes radiologists who can read and understand the manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Knowledge: User must be read and understand user manual... Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: ADD is not disposable, invasive, or implantable.
evidence.evidenceText: It is not disposable device... It is not invasive device... It is not implantable device

evidence.claim: ADD uses TFT glass, CsI scintillators, and amorphous silicon thin film technology.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD detector utilize a combination of propriety TFT glass and scintillators (CsI)... fabricated using thin film technology based on amorphous silicon technology.

evidence.claim: ADD supports auto-trigger signal sensing technology allowing use without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto-trigger signal sensing technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The plan states safety follows EN 60601-1, EMC follows EN 60601-1-2, and digital X-ray imaging function follows EN 62220-1-1.
evidence.evidenceText: For the safety of the device, the device is followed by (EN 60601-1), For electromagnetic compatibility, the device is followed by (EN 60601-1-2), For the digital X-ray imaging devices function followed by (EN 62220-1-1).

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation is intended to evaluate ADD clinical benefits compared to potential risks and is part of risk management under EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to evaluate the clinical benefits of the ADD in comparison to its potential risks. Therefore, this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2012.

evidence.claim: The literature search period is 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15 and was to use PUBMED and Science Direct.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” are going to utilize... Date of Search: 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the source text is primarily CE/MDD focused and cites MDD 93/42/EEC, Directive 2007/47/EC, notified bodies, competent authorities, and CE marking.
Source metadata productSignal includes HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document text identifies the model name as ADD and does not state HAD/A/F 1417-1717.
Sales approval status and multiple post-market data fields are listed as TBD.
Appendix II Clinical Literature lists Literature #1, #2, and #3 without titles or details in the provided text.
The document is a clinical evaluation plan with several forward-looking sections, not a completed clinical evaluation report.
